commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR enables Claude to select Session + Weekly as a menu-bar metric, renders combined session/weekly text with weekly pace, adjusts highest-usage behavior, and adds focused tests plus changelog text.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection for the blocking edge case: existing Claude web coverage shows `five_hour: null` plus `seven_day`, while the PR head accepts any 300-minute window as the combined session lane.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow code/test repair can address the blocking edge case without a maintainer-only design decision.

target.title: Claude: add session usage + weekly pace menu bar metric
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the combined **session usage + weekly pace** menu-bar metric option to the **Claude** provider, mirroring the existing Codex `Session + Weekly` option. The menu bar shows the 5-hour session and weekly lanes together (e.g. `5h 12% · W 45%` in percent mode) and paces on the weekly lane in pace/both modes.

Previously this combined option was gated to Codex only (`menuBarMetricSupportsPrimaryAndSecondary == .codex`), so Claude's *Menu bar metric* picker only offered Automatic / Primary (Session) / Secondary (Weekly) — even though Claude already exposes both lanes in its data, dual-lane icon, and dropdown rows.

To use it: **Preferences → Providers → Claude → Menu bar metric → Session + Weekly**.

## Changes

- Enable `.primaryAndSecondary` for Claude (`SettingsStore+MenuPreferences`), which surfaces the picker option, persistence, and read-back.
- Render the combined menu-bar text and weekly pace for Claude (`StatusItemController+Animation`).
- Rename the now-shared helper `MenuBarDisplayText.codexCombinedPercentText` → `combinedSessionWeeklyPercentText` (and `codexSessionLabel` → `sessionWindowLabel`), since it is no longer Codex-specific.
- Extend the "show highest usage" merged-icon exclusion for the combined metric to Claude (`UsageStore+HighestUsage`).
- Changelog entry under 0.37.3.

## Correctness note

Claude's OAuth usage can omit `five_hour`; in that case the existing mapper parks the 7-day window in **both** `primary` and `secondary`. A naive port would mislabel that as a session lane and render `168h 42% · W 42%`. The combined path therefore classifies lanes by **window cadence** (only a 300-minute window counts as the session lane), so a weekly-only account correctly shows `W 42%`. Codex keeps resolving its lanes through its consumer projection, unchanged.

## Tests

Added coverage for: the picker option appearing for Claude, the combined display (`5h 12% · W 45%`), the weekly-only fallback (`W 42%`), pace mode (weekly lane), and highest-usage ranking (one lane vs both lanes exhausted). `make check` is clean (0 lint violations); all directly-affected suites pass and the project builds in release.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: shengqiang-zhang
